### Post by shaheru on 2009-07-24
Hello!
I get some troubles to visualize an image file, and apparently
this should be resolve if I pass from 16 to 24 bits.
If I type this command:

sudo gedit /etc/X11/xorg.conf

the file is empty. How can I fill it and what should I write in it?

Thanks!

---

### Post by wojox on 2009-07-24
Here's the generic:

```
# xorg.conf.failsafe (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Configured Video Device"
	Driver		"vesa"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Configured Monitor"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Monitor		"Configured Monitor"
	Device		"Configured Video Device"
EndSection
```

---

### Post by shaheru on 2009-07-24
Ok, thanks for the file,
Do you know yet where I have to change the depth from 16 to 24?
Such files are quite confusing for me!!

---

### Post by kerry_s on 2009-07-24
if you boot into failsafe & run-> **X -configure**
you will get a xorg.conf.new made for your hardware, the file will be in /root you just copy that to /etc/X11/xorg.conf

**cat /root/xorg.conf.new > /etc/X11/xorg.conf**

in that one should have a setting for "DefaultDepth" if not post it here & i'll show where to put it.

---
